mode_selection_reason: Pre-flight directive discovery surfaced DIRECTIVE-20260505-001 with status:pending, priority:high, target_mode:Mode 4. Per dispatcher rules, directive overrides Mode 1–4 trigger logic and routes to the directive's target_mode. Skipped Mode 1/2/3 quick-checks for this run.

directive_handled.title: Audit workspace-sync smart_copy iteration list for gaps; primary case: processed-reviews.json missing → digest-reviews.js sees stale 'already_processed' set → decider re-processes the same reviews each run
directive_handled.directive_lifecycle_additive_edit_applie: status: pending→completed; appended completed_at, completed_by_run, prop_id_authored, amendment_file_authored. No other fields mutated.

previous_followup: Skipped this run. Directive override consumed the full window; PROP lifecycle verification deferred to next normal-mode run. Note: this run did NOT walk monitor/tinker/proposals/ to mark fixed/still-broken statuses — operator should not interpret silence as 'all PROPs verified.'

findings.title: monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json not in workspace-sync smart_copy iteration; FUSE +55h ahead of main
findings.evidence: FUSE last_updated=2026-05-05T13:24Z (451 entries); main last_updated=2026-05-03T05:21Z (447 entries); 4 missing entries match exactly the WIN-021.c3, WIN-014.c5, WIN-016.c6, WIN-017.c4 reviews that decider keeps flagging as 'verified stale' in daily-reports.
findings.root_cause: Enumeration omission in monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md Step 2. The decider clone-side write reaches FUSE via universal-pusher on 403, but workspace-sync's smart_copy iteration never picks it up to round-trip back to git.
findings.blast_radius: ~24 redundant Opus staleness checks per day; ~70 over the 3-day FUSE-vs-main staleness window. Daily-report 'verified stale' commentary clutters operator attention.
findings.proposed_fix: PROP-017 — add smart_copy line for processed-reviews.json plus 12 sibling fixes.

findings.title: 11 additional gap files surfaced by audit (same FUSE-newer-than-git pattern)
findings.evidence: monitor/curmudgeon/alerts.txt +98h, monitor/curmudgeon/latest-review-summary.txt +108h, monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json +99h, monitor/analyst/processed-proposals.json +60h, monitor/integrity/prop-009-shadow.jsonl +81h. Plus 6 'untracked-on-main' files (integrity/alerts.txt, integrity/latest-integrity-summary.txt, social/report-*.json, social/latest-summary.txt, analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt) that have never reached git.
findings.root_cause: Same enumeration-omission pattern as primary case. Each file is agent-written, not in NEVER_PUSH, but not enumerated in smart_copy or sync_glob.
findings.blast_radius: Decider intake reads stale alerts/summaries; tinker's PROP-009 shadow audit sees stale shadow log; analyst's attention-inbox round-trip is broken; daily social reports never reach git history.

findings.title: Enumeration approach is fundamentally fragile; same gap class will recur
findings.evidence: 12+ confirmed gaps across 5 directories. The pattern is: 'agent adds new write path → forgets to add line to workspace-sync.md → file silently strands on FUSE → invisible until someone investigates a stale-loop symptom downstream.' This is the second instance of this bug class (cf. PROP-016 source-file rescue gap).
findings.root_cause: Three-way duplication of file ownership classifications across build.js OWNERSHIP, workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH, and CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules. Adding a new write path requires manual updates in all three; gaps go undetected for days.
findings.proposed_fix: Deferred follow-ups: (a) sync-manifest pattern to consolidate the three-way duplication; (b) integrity-side gap detector that flags FUSE-newer-than-main files not classified by workspace-sync. Both are documented in PROP-017 follow_up_recommendations but not bundled into the immediate fix.

Comparing against the audit's confirmed gaps: at least 5 currently-agent-written files (processed-reviews.json, attention-inbox.json, processed-proposals.json, prop-009-shadow.jsonl, monitor/social/report-*.json) are not classified in the table. This is in addition to the PROP-017 workspace-sync fix and is a separate cleanup that the next CLAUDE.md audit should pick up.
